notes:
PRODUCTS (verified): Bulbapedia confirms Hidden Fates had NO traditional 36-pack booster box ("Hidden Fates booster packs could not be purchased individually from booster boxes"; sold only via ETBs/tins/collections) -> displayPacks=null. ETB = 10 packs, confirmed by the official Pokemon.com ETB product listing (10 Hidden Fates booster packs) -> etbPacks=10. Pack = 10 modeled cards + 1 basic Energy (energy not modeled, no value).

AVAILABLE BUCKETS (verified against snapshot.sets["hidden-fates"]): {common:18, uncommon:17, rare:21, ultra-rare:12, secret-rare:9, shiny-rare:86}. There is NO "shiny-vault" bucket for this SM-era set; all 86 priced Shiny Vault cards are bucketed under "shiny-rare", so ALL Shiny Vault pulls are summed onto "shiny-rare" (correct per this set). Full-art trainers Giovanni's Exile / Jessie & James sit in ultra-rare; rainbow Tapu-GX + TAG TEAM secret sit in secret-rare -- consistent with the coarse TCGdex mapping.

RARE-OR-BETTER (main set) -- CORRECTED. A real per-set aggregation now anchors this: ThePriceDex Hidden Fates gives Rare Holo 1/5, Rare Holo GX 1/6.7, Ultra Rare (full art) 1/25.6, Rainbow Rare 1/90.9. Coarse mapping: ultra-rare = P(GX 1/6.7)+P(FA 1/25.6) = 0.1493+0.0391 = 0.1883; secret-rare = P(rainbow 1/90.9 ~0.011) + a small gold/secret allowance = 0.017; rare = remainder 0.7947. This REPLACES the draft's grossly hot rare slot (draft had ultra-rare 0.26 = 1-in-4 packs and secret-rare 0.08 = 1-in-12.5 packs, implying ~9 GX and ~3 secrets per 36 packs -- contradicted by every source; SM boxes average ~4-5 ultra rares and ~1 secret per 2 boxes). Corrected values match "1 guaranteed FA, avg 4-5 URs/box" and rainbow ~1 in 2 boxes.

SHINY VAULT slot (value driver) -- CORRECTED to 0.33 (was 0.30). ThePriceDex: Shiny Rare (SV holo) 1/4.7 = 0.2128, Shiny Rare GX 1/10.6 = 0.0943, SV Ultra Rare 1/84, SV Secret 1/63; summed SV-card-per-pack presence ~0.335. Corroborated by Cardrake "SV-GX ~1 in 4 packs (25%)" and the Elite Fourum 1000-pack break (most individual SV cards pulled 0-1x across 1000 packs, confirming SV cards are per-pack scarce, NOT the absurd Cardrake "3-4 SV cards per pack" claim which is internally inconsistent and was discarded as unreliable AI-generated text). All SV hits map to shiny-rare = 0.33; filler common = 0.67.

CONFIDENCE raised low->medium: now backed by ThePriceDex per-set aggregation (same source family already trusted for shining-fates in this repo) plus two corroborating community sources, rather than a bare era-average.

INTEGRITY: every slot sums to exactly 1.0; all distribution keys are a strict subset of this set's available buckets {common, uncommon, rare, ultra-rare, secret-rare, shiny-rare}. The draft's top-level "displayPacks":"null" / "etbPacks":"10" string fields were malformed types -- dropped in favor of the typed products object (displayPacks:null, etbPacks:10).
